title: HBR-MAN-003 self-consistency scanner (manual names resolve to Rust symbols)
goal: Build `hbr-man-003-scan.mjs` per spec §10.15.5 (AC-MODEL-MANUAL-SELF-CONSISTENCY). Grep-scan Rust source for every command name, IPC channel id, schema field, config key, and CLI flag the manual names; a name in the manual that does not resolve to a Rust source symbol is an HBR-MAN-003 violation, preventing manual drift away from wired surface.
expected_diff_shape: New script .GOV/roles_shared/checks/hbr-man-003-scan.mjs; gov-check umbrella wiring; test fixture exercising pass/fail.
implementation_notes: Per spec §10.15.5 the scanner extracts every name from the manual and verifies each resolves to a Rust source symbol. Implementation: (1) Read src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_manual/content.rs and extract every CommandReference.name, .ipc_channel, .tauri_command, .schema_fields entries, and .cli_flag. (2) For each name, search the Rust source tree (src/backend/handshake_core/src/**/*.rs + app/src-tauri/src/**/*.rs) via ripgrep. Resolution rules: ipc_channel string must appear as a string literal somewhere; tauri_command must appear in an `#[tauri::command] pub fn <name>` signature; schema_field must appear as a `pub <name>:` field in some struct; cli_flag must appear as a clap attribute literal. (3) Names that fail to resolve are emitted as JSONL `{manual_id, name, kind, reason: "no_source_match"}` to stderr and exit code is 2. (4) Manual entries explicitly tagged `status: "planned"` (a new optional field on CommandReference added in this MT to types.rs — coordinate with MT-010 author if MTs run in parallel) are skipped per HBR-MAN-003 not_applicable_when. Caveat: ripgrep search must use word boundaries to avoid spurious substring matches; document the regex strategy in a comment block. Justfile: add `hbr-man-003` recipe and wire into gov-check. Tests: fixture manual with one bogus name fails; same fixture with the bogus name tagged status=planned passes; fixture with all real names against the live content.rs passes.
risk_if_missed: Without self-consistency, manual entries drift to reference renamed/removed Rust symbols; operator and no-context models follow stale documentation.
validator_focus: False positives are bounded: word-boundary regex prevents substring matches; status=planned escape hatch is documented and tested.

minimum_controls: status=planned escape hatch is tested (so future spec-deferred entries can survive scan).
Scanner exits non-zero on first unresolved name and prints ALL unresolved names (does not stop at first).
